title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Direct and in-depth. Interviews exploring the minds and motivations of the leaders, visionaries, artists and entrepreneurs who are changing our world.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: In squid-loving Japan, spear squid is immensely popular. Tag along with fishers in Hokkaido and savour some regional cuisine, fresh sushi, and a special take on a beloved Italian-inspired inky dish.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: A series of doodles by the famous artist Utagawa Kuniyoshi turn out to be carefully considered depictions of the kabuki actors of the day. We investigate what kind of artistic statement was he trying to make.

title: The Factory Wonders
subTitle: A selection of acclaimed programs from NHK's domestic channels dubbed into English.

title: Japan From Above: Up Close
subTitle: A journey along the renowned Tsugaru Railway in the snow-blanketed region of Aomori Prefecture in northeastern Japan.

title: Seasoning the Seasons
subTitle: Explores the streets of Kamakura in Kanagawa Prefecture, the first capital of Japan. Kamakura's popular attractions bring over 20 million tourists to the city every year.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Medical Frontiers
subTitle: Glaucoma, which can lead to blindness, often shows no symptoms in the early stages. We feature the latest efforts to restore the optic nerve, including new surgical techniques and gene therapy.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Where We Call Home
subTitle: People from around the globe who put down roots in Japan offer a glimpse into their lives and the hardships they face in Japanese society.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Activate Your Japanese
subTitle: Lessons on how the viewer can make the most out of their Japanese language skills. This time, how to navigate social situations in which everyone has a different opinion, including the viewer.

title: Culture Crossroads
subTitle: Bosai Leaders' promote mutual resident support before and during disasters, and these days foreign participants are playing a significant role. We introduce those being trained in Nakano Ward, Tokyo.

title: Hello! NHK WORLD-JAPAN
subTitle: Explores various aspects of Hokkaido, ranging from the ecology of brown bears and the craftsmanship of a bespoke bento to a landscape uniquely shaped by the region's rugged natural conditions and pioneering history.

title: Hello! NHK WORLD-JAPAN
subTitle: Explores various aspects of Hokkaido, ranging from the ecology of brown bears and the craftsmanship of a bespoke bento to a landscape uniquely shaped by the region's rugged natural conditions and pioneering history.

title: Science View
subTitle: Japanese swords are often called and treated as metal-made-art. The once lost techniques used to make these works of art prior to the 16th century are now on the verge of rediscovery.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Journeys in Japan
subTitle: James Lambiassi discovers Yamagata's long tradition of wooden architecture and contemporary uses for timber.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Grand Sumo Highlights
subTitle: Sumo highlights from a range of tournaments that have taken place across the globe. Sumo is one of the most traditional Japanese sports and boasts a history going back more than 1500 years.

title: Dig More Japan!
subTitle: International Women's Day aims for equality and an end to gender discrimination. We dig a little deeper into the choices facing modern women.

title: Dining with the Chef Mini
subTitle: Traditional techniques and resourceful recipes in just 5 minutes! Chefs Saito and Rika present their unique approaches to cooking delicious Japanese food.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Dining with the Chef
subTitle: Hosts Yu Hayami and Patrick Harlan, with the help of Japanese chefs Tatsuo Saito and Rika Yukimasa, introduce the uninitiated to the techniques, ingredients and harmony of Japanese cuisine.

title: Newsroom Tokyo
subTitle: Comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Yoshioka Hideto discusses his tenure leading the international NGO Japan Heart for over 20 years, providing free medical support in developing nations. He shares plans to establish a hub for paediatric care in Cambodia.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Yoshioka Hideto discusses his tenure leading the international NGO Japan Heart for over 20 years, providing free medical support in developing nations. He shares plans to establish a hub for paediatric care in Cambodia.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: Introducing asari, Japan's most popular clam. Visit Aichi to feast your eyes on asari dishes and see how they're cultivated using suspended cages. How about diving for some giant asari!?

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: We venture to the outskirts of Edo just after sunset, where a group of women and children have come to a stream to enjoy a popular summer activity. Find out what they are looking for amongst the weeds.

title: Demining Ukraine : Uncovering an Invisible Enemy
subTitle: As a large portion of Ukraine's territory remains contaminated by landmines, we follow a Japanese scientist's use of radar technology to support demining efforts in the war-torn country.

title: Document 72 Hours
subTitle: Three days in the life of...Stay in one location for 72 hours, catching glimpses into people's lives as they open up and share.
